requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Internal approval workflow is triggered after supplier onboarding initiation.
Approved supplier receives an invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps.
Supplier can register with AECI and complete due diligence steps via Coupa Supplier Portal.
Supplier information is accurate and current, and certifications and contact information are visible.
Multiple channels of managing supplier information are eliminated, and supplier participation is encouraged.
Supplier creation and onboarding process is managed by Coupa SIM, and a supplier is only created once they have completed the onboarding process.
Supplier status management includes purchase block, payment block, supplier deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows involve Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams.
Integration touchpoints between Coupa SIM and SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation are established.
Internal users can register a new supplier in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Buyers can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Users can update a supplier's information in Coupa, including status changes.
The One-Time Vendor (OTV) process enables faster vendor creation with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements, while ensuring basic compliance and procurement controls.
Coupa displays different status labels to help users quickly see the status of each supplier.
The system supports auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages.
Country-specific attachments and field requirements are controlled based on the selected country for the supplier.
